body.title: Rewrite high-friction skills + CLAUDE.md with binding imperatives + directed workflow + MCP-first
body.result: Rewrote 9 skills in ~/.claude/skills/ + CLAUDE.md + 8 palace rule nodes + WorkingWithClaude.md with binding imperative + directed workflow + MCP tool naming; mirrored to ClaudeAndAkien utility-closet variant (hard-rules pass). Commit ae1c0597 (TheIgors), 6726c88 (ClaudeAndAkien).
body.description:
High-friction skill prompts and CLAUDE.md rules currently state rules as negations ("do not skip X", "never Y"). This makes the right behavior a constraint to avoid instead of a path to follow. Rewrite mandatory-step lines using the paired shape: **binding imperative** (Always/Shall/Must/First as checklist anchor) + **directed workflow** (spell the path the right behavior travels) + **named MCP tool** (where a tool exists — prescribe the call, don't let ad-hoc psql/grep win by default). Rationale: CC reads imperative-led lines as required checkbox steps; workflow-phrased lines as a traversable path; tool-named steps as the frictionless default. All three layers required — imperative without workflow is a nag; workflow without imperative is ambient info; neither without the MCP reference when one applies means the ad-hoc path still wins. Commit per skill so reverts are cheap if a rewrite lands wrong.

**Affected files:** `/home/akien/.claude/skills/sprint/SKILL.md`, `/home/akien/.claude/skills/decided/SKILL.md`, `/home/akien/.claude/skills/ticket/SKILL.md`, `/home/akien/.claude/skills/review/SKILL.md`, `/home/akien/.claude/skills/context-load/SKILL.md`, `/home/akien/.claude/skills/savestateauto/SKILL.md`, `/home/akien/.claude/skills/fixit/SKILL.md`, `/home/akien/.claude/skills/sprint-batch/SKILL.md`, `/home/akien/.claude/skills/day-close/SKILL.md`, `/home/akien/TheIgors/CLAUDE.md`, palace nodes under `theigors/rules/*` (via palace_write or direct psql UPDATE).
**Design rules:** docs-in-code (skills are the docs — edit them, don't write a parallel style guide); scaffold-not-correct (rules visible at point-of-use in each skill, not remembered across sessions); test-plan-or-why-not; no-sqlite: no persistence — this ticket edits prose/markdown files only, no DB reads or writes.
**Scope boundary:** IN — rewriting existing mandatory-step lines in listed skills + CLAUDE.md + palace rules using the paired shape; naming MCP tools where one applies cleanly. OUT — adding new skills or new rules; rewriting advisory/informational lines (imperatives lose force if overused); building new MCP tools (the existing shelf covers the needs we've surfaced); changing skill behavior (pure prompt rewrite, not flow changes).
**Test plan:** no unit tests (prose). Verification: after rewriting each skill, follow it as-written on a small real task and note where the imperative/workflow/MCP-name triplet still left ambiguity. Iterate within the same ticket. One commit per skill.
